What is the time zone of Salt Lake City? Salt Lake City, the capital and most populous city of the state of Utah, operates on Mountain Standard Time (MST), which is seven hours behind Coordinated Universal Time (UTC-7). During Daylight Saving Time, which typically runs from early March to early November, Salt Lake City observes Mountain Daylight Time (MDT), moving its clock one hour ahead to UTC-6. This time zone arrangement aligns Salt Lake City with other major cities in the Mountain Time Zone, such as Denver, Colorado, and Phoenix, Arizona, although Phoenix does not observe Daylight Saving Time.

Parts of other states also fall within this timezone, including the western portions of Nebraska and Kansas, the southwestern corner of South Dakota, and the panhandle of Oklahoma.
